title: eriophyllum lanatum var. achilleoides
intro: Eriophyllum lanatum var. achilleoides, also known as Woolly Sunflower, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Woolly Sunflower include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions. Woolly Sunflower can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in salves and ointments to aid in the healing process.
